title: Carex × senayana
taxonomy: Carex × senayana is a hybrid sedge, resulting from a cross between Carex aquatilis and Carex flava. It belongs to the family Cyperaceae, which includes various types of sedges and rushes. The exact classification of Carex × senayana may vary depending on the source, but it is generally considered to be a member of the genus Carex, which comprises over 1,600 species of sedges. Carex × senayana is often classified as a cultivar or a hybrid within the species Carex aquatilis or Carex flava. The classification of Carex × senayana is used to identify and distinguish it from its parent species and other similar sedges.
